title: Weekend Wrap: Dow's Worst Weekly Streak Since 2023
commentary: Wall Street limps into the weekend with Dow notching its longest losing streak since October 2023—down 0.5% to 45,809 as Middle East turmoil and sticky inflation keep bulls sidelined. Super Micro's 26% implosion on fraud allegations caps a brutal week for tech; Nasdaq sheds another 1%. Oil consolidates near $97 as geopolitical risk premium persists; gold holds $4,570 after this week's historic 6% single-day plunge. BTC flat at $69,800, proving more resilient than equities amid the risk-off rout.

title: Close: Gold Recoils, Equities End Week Lower
commentary: Gold surrenders earlier gains, sliding to $4,570 (-0.8%) as profit-taking hits after week's wild swings—Super Micro -28% post-indictment caps ugly week for tech. S&P closes at 6,554 (-0.8%), Nasdaq -1.1% with Dow off 0.6%; quad witching expiry adds to chop. Oil firms to $96.67 (+1.2%) as geopolitical premium persists; BTC holds $69,783 flat on the day, shrugging off equity weakness. Dollar climbs to 99.71 as week ends risk-off.

title: Late Morning: Tech Bleeds, Super Micro Cratered
commentary: Tech leads the selloff with Nasdaq -1.2% as Super Micro crashes 27% after co-founder indictment—Iran war uncertainty keeps a lid on risk appetite despite Fed Waller dovish signals. Gold surges to fresh highs at $4,646 (+0.9%) as safe-haven bid returns; oil holds firm at $95.83 near triple-digit territory. BTC flat at $69,909 as crypto decouples from equity weakness; 10Y yields jump 5bp to 4.33% on stubborn inflation data.

title: US Open: Markets Attempt Rebound After Brutal Week
commentary: Dow opens at 46,021 attempting recovery after plunging to new 2026 lows—stagflation fears ease slightly as oil pulls back from $100+ extremes. Asia took it on the chin overnight: Nikkei crashed 3.4%, Hong Kong -0.9% as Iran conflict fallout ripples through. Crypto finds footing with BTC +1.4% at $70,327, outperforming risk assets as deleveraging exhausts. Traders eyeing quad witching expiration for potential volatility surge.

title: Overnight Update
commentary: Markets pause overnight after volatile session as traders await fresh catalysts. Equity futures drift sideways while commodities consolidate recent moves. Crypto markets stabilize near support levels as volatility subsides. Asian session expected to take cues from late US trade.

title: Asian Session Update
commentary: Markets trading mixed in Asian session as investors digest recent economic data. Tech stocks showing resilience while commodities consolidate near recent highs. Crypto markets finding support after recent volatility.

title: After Hours Update
commentary: Markets wrapped Wednesday's session in mixed fashion as tech led gains while commodities cooled. Fed policy signals continue to anchor expectations with traders pricing steady rates through mid-year. Gold retreated from recent highs on dollar strength while crude oil consolidated near $75 amid OPEC+ production uncertainty.

title: Close: Precious Metals Bloodbath Caps Brutal Week
commentary: Gold crashes 6.4% to $4,585 in worst single-day rout since 2013 as margin liquidations cascade—silver implodes nearly 10% amid flight from leveraged commodity longs. Oil holds at $100.56 (+4.4%) as Iran conflict intensifies; WTI briefly tagged $119 intraday after Qatar LNG facility strikes. Equities end red with Dow -0.7%, S&P -0.5% as Fed signals no 2026 cuts. Crypto bleeds alongside risk: BTC -2.8% at $69,414, Extreme Fear at 23.

title: Late Session: Selloff Deepens, BTC Breaks $70K Support
commentary: Equities extend losses into late session with Dow -0.8%, S&P -0.6% as oil spike to $97 keeps pressure on risk assets—Micron's post-earnings plunge adds to tech malaise. Precious metals carnage continues: gold at $4,597 (-2.8%), silver in freefall at $69.90 (-13%) as margin liquidation cascades. Bitcoin breaks critical $70K support, trading at $69,453 as Iran conflict escalation and fading rate-cut hopes hammer crypto sentiment.

title: Midday Meltdown: Precious Metals Crater on Margin Calls
commentary: Gold plunges nearly 7% to $4,561 in worst single-day drop since 2013 as margin liquidations cascade through commodity markets—silver follows with 12.4% crash to $68. Equities extend post-Fed losses with Dow -0.5%, S&P -0.6% as oil volatility and Powell's inflation warnings keep buyers sidelined. Dollar weakens to 99.7 as safe-haven flows shift to cash. BTC outperforms risk assets at $69,940, holding above key support while TradFi deleverages.
